title: dianthus collinus var. scaber
intro: Dianthus collinus var. scaber, also known as the alpine pink or fringed pink, is a low-growing, flowering plant that belongs to the carnation family. This herb has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and skin conditions. The health benefits of Dianthus collinus var. scaber include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate digestive issues, and provide relief from respiratory problems. The plant can be prepared as a tea or infused oil, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions, or taken internally to alleviate various health issues.
